candidates.title: Gradient-to-Weight Ratio Homeostasis: A Unified Feedback Control Framework and Diagnostic Benchmark for Dynamic Weight Decay
candidates.summary: All major dynamic WD methods are special cases of a single PID-style feedback control law that regulates the per-layer gradient-to-weight ratio rho_t toward a target trajectory rho*(t) = eta_t / tau. UDWDC algorithm with proportional control, prescriptive target trajectory, and three standardized evaluation metrics (BEM, CSI, AIS). Extended theoretical analysis: contraction-rate separation theorem, geometry-corrected alignment principle, layer-differentiated equilibria.
hypotheses: H1: Unified control law subsumes CWD, SWD, CPR, Defazio as special-case gain settings
H2: Theory-derived rho*(t) = eta_t/tau matches or exceeds grid-searched fixed WD
H3: Alignment signal quality is batch-size-dependent; binary CWD preferred at b<=256
H4: Alignment-modulated WD is budget-efficient when Var[delta_t] > 0
H5: Per-layer r* anti-correlates with per-layer delta* under alignment-modulated WD
H6: Average alignment predicts generalization better than worst-case alignment
H7: Temporal predictability gate - alignment carries genuine geometric information
candidates.pilot_focus: Framework validated: 7/7 unit tests pass, 7/7 methods run, ImageNet data loads. Next: CIFAR full comparison + temporal predictability gate.
key_strengths: Grounded in proven results (Defazio steady-state, Wang-Aitchison EMA scaling)
Computationally efficient: only per-group norms, no SVD
Prescriptive: rho*(t) derivable from first principles, zero new hyperparameters
Strong unifying power across 4 sub-traditions with clean parameter mapping table
Multiple falsifiable predictions enabling rigorous validation
Fairest comparison to date: all methods under matched conditions
key_risks: PID analogy may be descriptive but not prescriptive
Continuous alignment noisy at small batch (addressed by batch-size conditioning)
Budget-efficiency theorem depends on alignment variance > 0
CPR may be functionally equivalent and hard to beat

perspectives_weighted.pragmatist: HIGH - proportional-only controller for simplicity, practical benchmark focus, why-weight-decay repo as base
perspectives_weighted.contrarian: HIGH - batch-size noise analysis shapes algorithm design, CWD ablation controls (random-mask, halved-WD)
perspectives_weighted.empiricist: HIGH - pre-registered falsification criteria, temporal predictability gate, matched-conditions comparison
perspectives_weighted.theoretical: MEDIUM - contraction-rate separation theorem, geometry-corrected alignment principle, per-layer equilibria
perspectives_weighted.innovator: MEDIUM - PID framework concept, Fisher-weighted WD reserved as Alternative 2
perspectives_weighted.interdisciplinary: LOW - bio-inspired noise-robust design reserved as Alternative 4

candidates.title: Spectral-Homeostatic Weight Decay via Renormalization Group Flow
candidates.summary: Two-factor WD strategy: temporal schedule driven by spectral condition number kappa(t) via RG flow, and per-parameter modulation via homeostatic alignment. Independent of PID unification and alignment SNR.
hypotheses: Spectral condition number kappa(t) drives an optimal non-monotone WD schedule
The two factors (temporal + per-parameter) are substantially independent (correlation < 0.5)
Non-monotone schedule outperforms monotone on ResNet-50/ImageNet
candidates.pilot_focus: Track kappa(t) during ResNet-50/ImageNet training under standard schedules.
key_strengths: Higher raw novelty, fewer close prior works
SVD signal fundamentally different from alignment
Survives if alignment signal is uninformative
key_risks: Non-monotone schedule may not hold empirically

candidates.title: Fisher-Informed Weight Decay: Geometry-Aware Regularization via Natural Gradient Alignment
candidates.summary: Apply WD as natural gradient: scale per-parameter decay by inverse Fisher information (1/sqrt(v_t) in Adam). Zero computational overhead. Clean information-geometric motivation. WARNING: FAdam (2024, arXiv:2405.12807) already conceptually proposes Fisher-weighted WD as natural gradient. EWC (2017) uses Fisher-diagonal per-parameter decay. Novelty significantly reduced upon thorough search.
hypotheses: Fisher-weighted WD outperforms uniform WD by 0.2%+ on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet
Fisher heterogeneity across parameters correlates with improvement magnitude
Fisher-weighted WD reduces or eliminates the cold posterior effect
candidates.pilot_focus: Measure Fisher information heterogeneity across parameters in ResNet-20 and ViT-S/16.
candidates.novelty_revised_reason: FAdam (2024) already proposes Fisher-weighted natural-gradient WD; EWC (2017) uses Fisher-diagonal per-parameter decay. Core idea is NOT genuinely unexplored. Revised from 9 to 6.
key_strengths: Clean theoretical motivation from information geometry + Bayesian shrinkage
Isolated ablation study of FAdam's WD component could still be valuable
key_risks: FAdam (2024) substantially anticipates the core idea -- CRITICAL collision
EWC (2017) establishes Fisher-diagonal per-parameter decay mechanism
Noisy v_t may cause over-decay on rare features
Improvement magnitude may be too small to detect
James-Stein connection is suggestive but not watertight

candidates.title: Does Gradient-Weight Alignment Carry Marginal Information for Weight Decay? A Pre-Registered Falsification Study
candidates.summary: Purely empirical contribution: pre-registered falsification study testing whether alignment carries genuine geometric information for WD decisions. First quantitative AIS measurement. Temporal predictability gate. CWD vs random-mask vs halved-WD ablation.
hypotheses: Average alignment predicts generalization better than worst-case alignment
Alignment is NOT fully time-recoverable (temporal predictability gate)
CWD outperforms both random-50%-mask and halved-WD (alignment IS informative)
key_strengths: Stands regardless of theory: valuable positive or negative
Pre-registered design with explicit falsification criteria
No wasted compute -- same experiments as front-runner
key_risks: Effect sizes may be too small to detect

synthesis_metadata.key_decision: Front-runner is cand_udwdc (PID unification) with pragmatist simplification: proportional-only controller, zero new hyperparameters. Innovation candidates (Fisher WD, spectral-homeostatic) reserved as high-novelty backups. Falsification study integrated into front-runner as ablation experiments.

perspectives_weighted_most.reason: Proportional-only controller, matched-conditions benchmark, implementation feasibility assessment -- shaped the algorithm design to be minimal and the paper structure to be robust regardless of whether UDWDC beats CWD
Batch-size noise analysis fundamentally shapes algorithm design (binary vs continuous recommendation), CWD ablation controls (random-mask, halved-WD) are the critical experiments
Pre-registered falsification criteria, temporal predictability gate, matched-conditions comparison, and statistical methodology define the evaluation rigor

perspectives_partially_integrated.reason: Contraction-rate separation theorem and geometry-corrected alignment integrated. Jensen direction issue acknowledged and scoped to stagewise schedules.
perspectives_partially_integrated.what_was_dropped: Full Lyapunov analysis for per-step adaptive lambda_t (too restrictive assumptions); Norm-geometry duality for Muon/Shampoo (scope limited to Adam)

perspectives_partially_integrated.reason: Fisher-weighted WD elevated to Alternative 2 (highest novelty backup). Thermodynamic analysis downgraded from core contribution to discussion/future work -- intractable entropy estimation makes it non-load-bearing.
perspectives_partially_integrated.what_was_dropped: Multi-scale Lyapunov WD (prohibitive SVD cost, CPR already occupies the niche)

perspectives_partially_integrated.reason: Dead-zone and Hill-function cooperativity reserved as Alternative 4 (noise-robust design). The mechanostat dead zone and quorum sensing cooperativity are genuinely novel but add too many hyperparameters for the front-runner.
perspectives_partially_integrated.what_was_dropped: Dissipative structures interpretation (decorative, reduces to Defazio's known results)
